text:
Lansing native Kyle Dake secured his second consecutive Olympic bronze medal on Saturday, rallying late to defeat Serbia’s Khetik Tsabolov 10-4 in the 74 kg wrestling weight class at the Paris Olympics. Dake, a four-time World Champion, expressed gratitude to his supporters in a post-match video, acknowledging the challenges he faced this year, including the loss of his father.

In Lansing, a tight-knit community gathered at the local Community Center to cheer on their hometown hero. Despite a semifinal loss to Japan’s Daichi Takatani, Dake’s resilience and determination were met with admiration and applause from the crowd.

Dake’s bronze adds to his 2020 Tokyo Olympic success, further cementing his legacy as one of Lansing’s most celebrated athletes.

title: Lansing's Kyle Dake clinches second straight Olympic bronze medal
